creator: inbox@alleghenycomputer.com
text:
Just an FYI... I tried installing newsboat outside of portmaster using:

cd /usr/ports/www/newsboat/ && make install clean

and got the same results as I did with portmaster.  So, whatever is happening it does not appear to be portmaster's fault.  I mention this  as someone changed the title of this report to include "portmaster"; which was not me.

Also...

I had a reason to install weechat this evening (first time) and it also failed with more or less the same line near just before it error-ed out:

"asciidoctor: not found"

I have rubygem-asciidoctor installed (v2.0.10) and it shows in the beginning during the dependency check (beginning of my attached file).

Maybe this has nothing to do with my problem as I am only about 3 years into running an office "fully" on FreeBSD and am certainly newer at this than many.  So, I apologize if this finding is meaningless.

I am just adding this info in case it "might" help in some way.  As to my newbie eyes it looks like both of these programs are tripping over the same problem.

I also have Ranger and CMUS installed (other cmd line programs) and they are working just fine.
